mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately suppress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate earnings and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in the industrial production index during post-shock recovery phases reflect rapid capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum loses predictive power for 5-day returns outside recovery regimes, or the signal generates negative alpha during normal expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 310.0, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and forces negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the inflation threshold shows no incremental predictive power over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 earnings that materializes over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rate shows a negative or null correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that trigger monetary policy uncertainty, suppressing S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to explain variance in 20-day returns, or the signal predicts positive returns when inflation outpaces growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that erode corporate margins and trigger negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the regime-specific signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward validation.
